articles.summary: Iran claims attacks on Amazon infrastructure in Bahrain Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said it carried out strikes on infrastructure belonging to the multinational company Amazon in Bahrain with "several cruise missiles". articles.title: US hits civilian area in Iran's Abdanan, state media reports
articles.summary: US hits civilian area in Iran's Abdanan, state media reports Iranian state media is reporting that the US targeted areas on the outskirts of the city of Abdanan. The governor of Abdanan, Behzad Noormohammadi, told IRNA that "the American enemy attacked a civilian point in the mountains around Abdanan by launching a missile". He indicated that the strikes did not result in any casualties, with an investigation into the incident ongoing.  "Relief agencies have been dispatched to the scene of the incident and are busy closely examining the dimensions of the enemy's aggression," he added

articles.title: IRGC targets more US army infrastructure in Kuwait
articles.summary: IRGC targets more US army infrastructure in Kuwait The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) it struck more US military targets in Kuwait, state news agency IRNA reported. According to the statement, drones, missile defence systems and radars as well as satellite reception systems in Kuwait were "hit by missiles and drones and destroyed". It added that an MQ9 drone hangar was also targeted at the Ali al-Salem base.

articles.title: Centcom says it ended latest round of attacks against Iran
articles.summary: Centcom says it ended latest round of attacks against Iran US Central Command (Centcom) said that it ended another wave of strikes against Iran, claiming attacks on military command centres, maritime capabilities, missile and drone launch sites as well as air defence systems. According to the statement, Centcom also said it helped "facilitate the transit of approximately 900 commercial vessels and 450 million barrels of crude oil".
